title: Any Escape
description: Understand the use (and risks) of the 'any' type when dealing with untyped or dynamic data.
problemStatement:
The wild card! Declare a variable `dynamicData` and set its type to `any`. Assign a string to it, then assign a number to it.

**Requirement:** Use the `any` keyword to opt-out of type checking for this variable.
hints: Using `any` basically turns off TypeScript's safety features for that variable.
expectedOutput: A variable that can hold both a string and a number
solutionCode:
let dynamicData: any = 'I am a string';
dynamicData = 42; // This is allowed because of 'any'
